based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/xor-per-neuron-bias.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted v0.4 canonical bytes, per-neuron biases on h1/h2/y with bias_policy.mode=sgd). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

mutation.delta_vs_tolerance_multiplier: ~970x (effective tolerance ~1.03e-9 at this magnitude under {atol: 1e-12, rtol: 1e-8}; delta ~1e-6 is well above the rtol-scaled noise floor)
what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[6].weight_before (0.100000000 — left operand of Rule 6 sum)
updates[6].update (0.00309395108 — right operand of Rule 6 sum)
updates[6].gradient + updates[6].optimizer.* (Rule 4 + Rule 5 still pass — gradient and update are internally consistent)
parameters_after.b_h1 (0.103093951 — derived from parameters_before + update; Rule 7 still passes because it uses STORED update, not STORED weight_after)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 6 failure naming updates[6].weight_after on parameter b_h1. Rule 5 must NOT fire (update is unchanged). Rule 7 must NOT fire (parameters_after.b_h1 is consistent with parameters_before + STORED update, which is the spec form of Rule 7).
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures. The fixture_status block being deliberately_corrupted / expected_to_fail_reconciliation must NOT mask the Rule 6 failure.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage) extended into the v0.4 per-neuron bias corner: proves the reconciler checks Rule 6 (weight_after == weight_before + update) on bias updates by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch between stored weight_after and the sum of (unmutated) weight_before + update. This is a clean single-rule fixture — no cascades — because Rule 6 reads stored weight_after but Rule 7 reads stored update.
v0_4_note: For bias updates, weight_before == parameters_before[bias_id] and weight_after represents the post-update bias value. Rule 6 fires identically whether the parameter is a weight or a bias.
